title: The Seasons Of Her Year
content:
I

    Winter is white on turf and tree,
     And birds are fled;
    But summer songsters pipe to me,
     And petals spread,
    For what I dreamt of secretly
     His lips have said!

II

    O 'tis a fine May morn, they say,
     And blooms have blown;
    But wild and wintry is my day,
     My birds make moan;
    For he who vowed leaves me to pay
     Alone - alone!
meaning:
Thomas Hardy's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Seasons Of Her Year"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
